params.name: Pnakotic Manuscripts
params.description:
On March 11, 1452, a European scholar discovered this ancient manuscript in his collection.
The Pnakotic Fragments was copied by an anonymous scholar from a Greek papyrus scroll, and its name and content are derived from the Lomar civilization of the first humans. It is said that the great winged ones visited to help them, and the identity of the winged ones has long been debated, but the symbols used in the book are the same as those used in the sea lily creatures of the ages, and it is said …
